divergences.description: agent_b treats CLAUDE/AGENTS operational detail exposure as blocker-level, while agent_c favors split handling (sanitize high-risk operational details aggressively, keep non-exploitable guidance lower-tier); agent_a is closer to full alignment but less explicit about split criteria.
agent_b maintains a full 19-issue release artifact, whereas agent_c emphasizes phase-gated execution and a tighter must-fix-now set; agent_a trends concise but with less exhaustive coverage detail.

agent_b keeps more branding/domain coupling in higher urgency tiers, while agent_c narrows high severity to runtime/deploy/security-impacting coupling and treats attribution-only references as non-blocking.

rationale: agent_b offers the strongest completeness, correction quality, and operational detail, while agent_a provides the cleanest concise synthesis for fast decision-making. All agents now align on core blockers (deployability lock-in, security disclosure, licensing clarity), but differ on severity thresholds for internal docs and branding-adjacent findings, and on how broad the final release artifact should be.
